mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in the effective federal funds rate tighten financial conditions and raise discount rates applied to equity cash flows, causing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward returns are non-negative during months when FEDFUNDS increases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8.0% signals persistent labor market slack that depresses consumer spending expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of triggered months when UNRATE exceeds 8.0% during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices and a falling federal funds rate signal stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and boosts equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction of rising CPIAUCSL and falling FEDFUNDS does not predict negative 20-day returns, measured by Rank IC above -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recoveries, month-over-month industrial production growth signals re-activation of capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Less than 55% positive 5-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases during post-crisis recovery years, or t-test p > 0.05 versus zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects robust consumer demand that translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months with rising RSAFS over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls declining by more than 100 thousand persons over a 3-month span signals contracting labor income and collapsing aggregate demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in greater than 50% of instances when PAYEMS declines by more than 100 thousand over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 750 thousand units signals contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in greater than 55% of months when HOUST is below 750 during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms the economy is expanding above stall speed, reducing recession probability and lifting equities over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 growth are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) or are negative over any 3-year rolling window.
